scope: M4 practical seamanship vocabulary expansion. Draft-only word, short-expression, and phrase items covering windward/leeward terms, tide/current vocabulary, harbour control areas, mooring/anchor commands, day shapes/lights, and boarding safety. Do not merge into playable starter or deploy to production in this sprint.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: lee shore / leeward side / windward shore
qa_notes.linguist_note: Lee shore is a hazard term and must not collapse into leeward side.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: windward / leeward; side / shore
qa_notes.linguist_note: Windward/leeward contrast is safety-relevant.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: leeward / windward; side / lee shore
qa_notes.linguist_note: Leeward side is not the same as lee shore.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: tidal stream / tidal height / traffic lane
qa_notes.linguist_note: Current direction/force vocabulary must not become tide height or traffic lane.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: ebb / flood / slack
qa_notes.linguist_note: Ebb/flood direction must be distinct.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: flood tide / ebb tide / flood warning
qa_notes.linguist_note: Flood tide is a tidal state, not a warning.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: fairway / gangway / runway
qa_notes.linguist_note: Fairway must not be confused with gangway or non-maritime runway.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: turning basin / waiting basin / traffic lane
qa_notes.linguist_note: Harbour manoeuvring area must remain exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: black ball / black cone / anchor light
qa_notes.linguist_note: Day shape vocabulary must not collapse into light or another shape.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: anchor light / black ball / deck light
qa_notes.linguist_note: Night light and day shape must stay distinct.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: keep clear / keep close / make fast
qa_notes.linguist_note: Clearance instruction must not reverse into close approach.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: hold / alter / report; position / course
qa_notes.linguist_note: Holding position is not reporting position or holding course.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: make fast / make way / cast off
qa_notes.linguist_note: Mooring command must not become movement or departure.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: single up / double up / let go; lines / anchor
qa_notes.linguist_note: Departure mooring preparation must keep line command exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: heave up / drop / hold; anchor / line
qa_notes.linguist_note: Anchor recovery must not become anchoring or line handling.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: anchor dragging / holding / heaving
qa_notes.linguist_note: Dragging anchor is an urgent status and must not become holding.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: engine standby / stopped / failed; engine / steering
qa_notes.linguist_note: Readiness must not collapse into failure or stop.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: steerage way / steering way / give way / underway
qa_notes.linguist_note: Steerage way is a handling term, not steering command or collision rule.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: safe / not safe; board / depart
qa_notes.linguist_note: Boarding permission must preserve safety polarity.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: not safe / safe; board / depart / disembark
qa_notes.linguist_note: Negative safety status must not be accepted as permission.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: keep clear / keep close / proceed; fairway / gangway
qa_notes.linguist_note: Fairway avoidance must not become approach or wrong area.

items.target_text: Hold position outside the turning basin.
accepted_answers: Hold position outside the turning basin.

should_accept: Hold position outside the turning basin.
should_reject: Hold position inside the turning basin
Report position outside the turning basin
Hold course outside the turning basin
dangerous_minimal_pairs: hold/report; position/course; outside/inside turning basin
qa_notes.linguist_note: Location and action must remain exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: single up / double up / let go; forward / aft lines
qa_notes.linguist_note: Line preparation must keep line group and action exact.

accepted_answers: Heave up the anchor when ready

should_accept: Heave up the anchor when ready
dangerous_minimal_pairs: heave up / drop / hold; anchor / line
qa_notes.linguist_note: Anchor recovery command must not become anchoring or line handling.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: anchor/line; dragging/holding; leeward/windward
qa_notes.linguist_note: Dragging direction and object are safety-critical.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: east/west; tidal stream/traffic stream/tidal height
qa_notes.linguist_note: Current direction and phenomenon must remain exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: ebb/flood; decreasing/increasing; depth/speed
qa_notes.linguist_note: Tide state and consequence must remain exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: flood/ebb/flood warning; increasing/decreasing current
qa_notes.linguist_note: Flood tide must not become flood warning or wrong current trend.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: hoist/lower; black ball/black cone/anchor light
qa_notes.linguist_note: Day shape command must keep action and shape exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: anchor light/black ball/deck light; visible/not visible
qa_notes.linguist_note: Light identity and visibility polarity must stay exact.

examples: Hold position outside the turning basin.

grammar_patterns.pattern: heave up anchor with readiness condition

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: lee shore / windward side / leeward side
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Wind-relative side and lee-shore hazard terms must remain distinct.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: ebb tide / flood tide / tidal stream
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Tide state, current, and warning terms must not collapse.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: fairway / turning basin / gangway; keep clear / hold position / proceed

phrase_vocab_hold_position_turning_basin: Hold position outside the turning basin.
Hold position inside the turning basin
Report position outside the turning basin

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Harbour area identity and movement instruction must stay exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: black ball / anchor light / black cone; hoist / lower / visible
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Day shapes and navigation lights have different action/status boundaries.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: make fast / single up / heave up / cast off
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Mooring and anchor commands are operationally different.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: anchor dragging / anchor holding / leeward / windward
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Dragging/holding and leeward/windward status are safety-critical.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: safe to board / not safe to board / safe to depart
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Boarding safety polarity must not be reversed.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: tidal stream east / west; ebb/flood trends
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Tide/current direction and trends must remain exact.

producer_notes.target_increment: 30 draft vocabulary units toward M4 1000+ items
producer_notes.next_gate: Sea Speak Linguist Review before engineering gate or merge
